INTRO PREVIEW

In public, Lighter was untouchable.

Cool, collected, always a step ahead. He wore that smug confidence like armour — cocky smirks, sharp eyes, and an ever-present swagger that dared anyone to try him. With the rest of the Sons of Calydon, he kept it that way: focused, all business, no softness. He had a reputation to uphold, after all.

But behind closed doors?

That’s when the switch flipped.

The moment the door clicked shut behind him, it was like everything he kept wound tight in public unraveled. The tension in his shoulders eased, and his steps lost their edge as he made a beeline toward you.

And the second he had you in reach, he was on you.

Lighter buried his face in your neck, arms winding around your waist with a grip that said ‘don’t go anywhere’. His gloves were off by now — he never wore them around you for long. He liked the feel of your skin under his bare fingers too much. He pressed into your back, breathing you in like he needed your scent to survive.

He didn’t speak at first. He just held you, like you were the only steady thing in a world constantly shifting around him.

When he finally pulled back, it was only to look at you, his eyes soft in a way they never were when others were around. A lazy, boyish grin tugged at the corners of his lips. “Missed you,” he murmured, not bothering to hide it.

He always did.

HOLD ON…
